sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.51 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: Given a prime \(p\) and \(n \), let \(f_p(n)\) be the smallest number such that there is a group of order \(p^{f_p(n)}\) into which every group of order \(p^n\) embeds. Is it true that \(f_p(n)\) grows faster than polynomially but slower than exponentially when \(n\) tends to infinity? { S.\,Kohl}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
